Lower bound of the severity scaling formula: frac=(score-lower)/(width); 0.3 is the elevated-band floor — below this disruption fraction should approach 0
Width of severity scaling: frac=(score-0.3)/width; 0.6 spans elevated→critical band so score=0.9 → frac=1.0
Sanctions factor score above which disruption_days is set longer; >0.5 means sanctions are a primary driver suggesting sustained supply loss
Default disruption duration when sanctions are the primary factor; sanctions-driven closures average 30 days before alternative routing
Default disruption duration for non-sanctions events (military exercises
column8: weather); shorter because physical events typically resolve faster

notes: Score below this threshold → escalating profile; above 0.8 → crisis is at peak so profile switches to constant (not still escalating)
Score at or above this → aggressive SPR policy; critical band (>=0.9) warrants maximum drawdown readiness
Hard cap on disruption_days sanity check; longest observed oil supply disruption (Libya 2011) was ~120 days; 180 provides headroom
Hard cap on demand_destruction_pct sanity check; NIPFP demand elasticity study bounds severe demand destruction at ~30%
